parameters.query:
WITH recent AS (
  SELECT
    id, session_id, created_at, message, lead_name, lead_phone,
    CASE WHEN message->>'type' = 'human' THEN COALESCE(message->>'content','') ELSE '' END AS human_row_text,
    COALESCE(
      NULLIF(lead_phone, ''),
      (SELECT regexp_replace((m)[1], '\D', '', 'g') FROM regexp_matches(CASE WHEN message->>'type' = 'human' THEN COALESCE(message->>'content','') ELSE '' END, '(?:\+?1[\s\-.]?)?(?:\(?\d{3}\)?[\s\-.]?)\d{3}[\s\-.]?\d{4}', 'g') AS t(m) LIMIT 1)
    ) AS row_extracted_phone,
    COALESCE(
      (SELECT (m)[1] FROM regexp_matches(CASE WHEN message->>'type' = 'human' THEN COALESCE(message->>'content','') ELSE '' END, '(?:\bthis\s+is|\bi''?m|\bi\s+am|\bthanks,\s*|\bregards,\s*)([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+){1,2})', 'i') AS t(m) LIMIT 1),
      (SELECT (m)[1] FROM regexp_matches(CASE WHEN message->>'type' = 'human' THEN COALESCE(message->>'content','') ELSE '' END, '\b([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+){1,2})\b', 'g') AS t(m) WHERE (m)[1] ~ '^[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+){1,2}$' LIMIT 1)
    ) AS row_name_guess
  FROM public.n8n_web_chat_histories
  WHERE created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '96 hours'
),
agg AS (
  SELECT session_id, MIN(created_at) AS first_message_at, MAX(created_at) AS last_message_at, COUNT(*) AS message_count,
    jsonb_agg(jsonb_build_object('id', id, 'created_at', created_at, 'message', message, 'lead_name', lead_name, 'lead_phone', lead_phone, 'extracted_phone', row_extracted_phone, 'name_guess', row_name_guess) ORDER BY created_at) AS messages,
    STRING_AGG(NULLIF(human_row_text, ''), ' ') AS human_text,
    MAX(NULLIF(lead_name, '')) AS lead_name_any,
    MAX(NULLIF(lead_phone, '')) AS lead_phone_any
  FROM recent GROUP BY session_id
),
derived AS (
  SELECT a.*,
    (SELECT regexp_replace((m)[1], '\D', '', 'g') FROM regexp_matches(COALESCE(a.human_text, ''), '(?:\+?1[\s\-.]?)?(?:\(?\d{3}\)?[\s\-.]?)\d{3}[\s\-.]?\d{4}', 'g') AS t(m) LIMIT 1) AS phone_from_text,
    (SELECT (m)[1] FROM regexp_matches(COALESCE(a.human_text, ''), '(?:\bthis\s+is|\bi''?m|\bi\s+am|\bthanks,\s*|\bregards,\s*)([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+){1,2})', 'i') AS t(m) LIMIT 1) AS name_from_text,
    (SELECT (m)[1] FROM regexp_matches(COALESCE(a.human_text, ''), '\b([A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+){1,2})\b', 'g') AS t(m) WHERE (m)[1] ~ '^[A-Z][a-z]+(?:\s+[A-Z][a-z]+){1,2}$' LIMIT 1) AS bare_name_candidate
  FROM agg a
)
SELECT d.session_id, d.message_count::text AS message_count,
  to_char(d.first_message_at AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.MS"Z"') AS first_message_at,
  to_char(d.last_message_at  AT TIME ZONE 'UTC', 'YYYY-MM-DD"T"HH24:MI:SS.MS"Z"') AS last_message_at,
  COALESCE(d.lead_name_any, d.name_from_text, d.bare_name_candidate) AS session_lead_name,
  COALESCE(d.lead_phone_any, d.phone_from_text) AS session_lead_phone,
  d.messages
FROM derived d ORDER BY d.last_message_at DESC LIMIT 200;

nodes.name: Get Chat History (last 48 hours)1
nodes.notes: Only retrieve chat histories and messages created in the last 96 hours (4 days).

parameters.query:
SELECT legacy_crm.dbo.Customers.InitialContact, legacy_crm.dbo.Customers.Email, legacy_crm.dbo.Customers.CustomerId
FROM legacy_crm.dbo.Customers
  WHERE legacy_crm.dbo.Customers.InitialContact = '{{$fromAI('name')}}'

SELECT *
FROM legacy_crm.dbo.ContactHistory
LEFT JOIN legacy_crm.dbo.Contacts
  ON legacy_crm.dbo.Contacts.ContactID = legacy_crm.dbo.ContactHistory.ContactID
WHERE legacy_crm.dbo.ContactHistory.ContactHistoryDate >= '{{ $json.timestamp_with_offset }}'

nodes.name: Insert into lostContact2
nodes.notes: If isResolved is BOOLEAN, change mapping to {{ false }}. Consider a unique index on (email, time, channel).

parameters.query:
SELECT id, session_id, created_at,
       (message->>'type') AS msg_type,
       (message->>'content') AS msg_content,
       lead_name, lead_phone
FROM public.n8n_web_chat_histories
WHERE created_at >= '{{ $json.startISO }}'::timestamptz
ORDER BY created_at DESC;

SELECT ch.ContactHistoryDate AS createdDateTime,
       c.FullName AS FullName,
       c.Email AS Email,
       ch.ContactNotes AS ContactNotes,
       ch.ContactType AS ContactType
FROM legacy_crm.dbo.ContactHistory ch
LEFT JOIN legacy_crm.dbo.Contacts c ON c.ContactID = ch.ContactID
WHERE ch.ContactHistoryDate >= '{{ $json.startISO }}'
ORDER BY ch.ContactHistoryDate DESC;

options.systemMessage:
You are an expert customer relations analyst and communication comparator. Your task is to analyze the incoming prompt data for incoming customer communications (chat threads, emails, outgoing emails, and CRM communications) and identify ALL unique contacts that may have been missed in follow-up responses within the last 96 hours.

## INPUT DATA FORMAT
You will receive:
1. Chat Threads - Array of chat session data with message history
2. Incoming Emails - Array of customer email communications
3. Outgoing Emails - Array of internal/external follow-up emails sent by your team
4. CRM Communications - Array of internal/external follow-up notes sent by your team
5. Timeframe Context - All communications are within the last 96 hours

## RESPONSE FORMAT
Return your findings as a structured JSON object:
{
  "analysis": [
    {
      "customerName": "Full Name of Customer (if available)",
      "emailAddress": "Customer email (if available)",
      "phoneNumber": "Customer phone (if available)",
      "contactTime": "Timestamp of original contact (YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ)",
      "channel": "Email/Chat/Both",
      "originalMessage": "Summary of original customer message",
      "status": "No Response Yet/Response Sent/Response Pending/Response Resolved",
      "followUpMessage": "Summary of follow-up response (if any)",
      "followUpTime": "Timestamp of follow-up response (if any)",
      "confidenceScore": 0.0,
      "reasoning": "Detailed explanation of your analysis"
    }
  ],
  "summary": {
    "totalContacts": 0,
    "noResponse": 0,
    "responseSent": 0,
    "responsePending": 0,
    "responseResolved": 0
  }
}

## IMPORTANT RULES
1. Time window: 96 hours
2. Match contacts by name, email, or phone (any one identifier)
3. Confidence: High (0.8-1.0) = clear follow-up found; Medium (0.5-0.7) = partial match; Low (0.0-0.4) = no match
4. Use Lookup tools when contact information is missing
5. Return valid JSON only - no markdown outside the JSON structure
6. Focus on No Response Yet status as highest priority
